behavior_summary: This script implements the player-facing description (U8P_MAGSCROL::look) for a magic scroll item and a helper 'U8P_MAGSCROL::use' process that launches the scroll's effect.

U8P_MAGSCROL::look reads the item's quality/type via Item::getQuality(this) and shows a short label (Bark) chosen from explicit cases (e.g., "default scroll", "scroll of healing", "scroll of invisibility"). If the quality code isn't recognized it falls back to a generic "magic scroll" label.

U8P_MAGSCROL::use reads the same quality/type value and spawns a scroll-effect process appropriate to that quality (various SCROLL1::... or SCROLL2::... entry points). If no explicit mapping matches, it falls back to spawning a default SCROLL1 effect.

Most detailed runtime behavior (what the scroll actually does) is implemented in the spawned SCROLL1/SCROLL2 functions; this script only maps quality codes to labels/effect entry points and launches them.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MAGSCROL::look: switch-like dispatch on Item::getQuality(this) -> choose a Bark string for specific quality codes; default case emits "magic scroll".
U8P_MAGSCROL::use: switch-like dispatch on Item::getQuality(this) -> spawn corresponding SCROLL1::funcXXXX or SCROLL2::funcXXXX entry point; default case spawns SCROLL1::func0080, then suspend/wait.
Both processes are short-lived: U8P_MAGSCROL::look emits a single bark and suspends/ends; U8P_MAGSCROL::use spawns an effect and then suspends, handing behavior to the spawned process.
open_questions: Are all quality codes handled in-game covered by the explicit cases shown, or do additional qualities exist that map to other SCROLL functions?
Do any spawned SCROLL1/SCROLL2 entry points require parameters or actor context beyond the item pointer passed here (inferred as addressof(this))?
Is there any additional state change (e.g., item consumption/destruction) performed by the spawned SCROLL entry points rather than by this script?

main_function.name: U8P_MAGSCROL::look
main_function.purpose: When the player examines the scroll item, read the item's quality/type and display a short descriptive label (Bark) appropriate to that scroll type; fall back to a generic label for unknown types.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player performs a 'U8P_MAGSCROL::look' or examine action on this scroll item (process-type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_MAGSCROL::look
function_map.role: Provide short player-facing description (Bark) for the scroll based on its Item::getQuality code.

function_map.name: U8P_MAGSCROL::use
function_map.role: Launch the scroll's effect by spawning the appropriate SCROLL1/SCROLL2 entry point based on Item::getQuality; acts as the 'U8P_MAGSCROL::use' handler for the item.

internal_calls.purpose: No evidence of a direct call; listed as a helper in the same script but not invoked by U8P_MAGSCROL::look in the recovered nodes.

external_calls.purpose: Retrieve the item's quality/type code used to select which short label (Bark) to show.
Retrieve the item's quality/type code used to select which scroll-effect entry point to spawn.
Create/launch a scroll-effect process/thread (calls various SCROLL1::funcXXXX or SCROLL2::funcXXXX entry points).
Default effect entry point spawned for unspecified or default quality codes (explicit mapping shows many SCROLL1::funcXXXX variants handled similarly).
Alternate effect entry point spawned for certain quality codes (explicit mapping uses SCROLL2 variants for other scroll types).
Mapped effect implementations for specific quality codes (examples: 0x32->SCROLL2::func0520, 0x34->SCROLL2::func0AF5, 0x34/0x35/0x36/0x37 etc.).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the player-facing description (U8P_MAGSCROL::look) for a magic scroll item and a helper 'U8P_MAGSCROL::use' process that launches the scroll's effect.
